data.title: Tourmaline Declared Quarterly Dividend
data.description:
Turmaline Oil Corp. announced overnight Wednesday that its Board of Directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend on its common shares of C$0.50 per share

In March 2025 the company, Canada's largest natural-gas producer, announced a quarterly base dividend increase of 43% to $0.50 per share effective from Q1 2025. That month it also announced a special dividend, which wasn't included in the most recent declaration.

data.title: Planet Labs gains after upbeat annual revenue forecast
data.description:
** Shares of satellite imagery company Planet Labs jump 24.1% to $4.95 premarket

** Company forecasts annual revenue in the range of $265 mln-$280 mln, with its mid-point above analysts' estimate of $270.6 mln - data compiled by LSEG

** Q1 revenue of $66.27 mln also above estimate of $62.3 mln

** Average rating of 11 analysts is a "buy"; median PT at $5 - data compiled by LSEG

** Stock down slightly YTD, till last close

data.title: Linklaters has advised the syndicate of banks in connection with Alvotech's private placement
data.description:
Linklaters

Linklaters has advised the syndicate of banks in connection with Alvotech's private placement of SDRs and ordinary shares by means of accelerated bookbuilding process conducted by DNB Carnegie and Citi as Joint Global Coordinators and Joint Bookrunners, and SEB and ACRO as Joint Bookrunners.

The transaction attracted strong interest from institutional investors and resulted in Alvotech raising gross proceeds of SEK 750 million. Alvotech was recently triple listed on Nasdaq Stockholm in addition to its listings on Nasdaq Global Market (New York) and Nasdaq Reykjavik, a listing in which Linklaters advised the banks as well.

"This transaction marks another significant milestone for Alvotech, reflecting our commitment to providing seamless support with complex capital markets issues. We are grateful for the trust placed in us by all parties involved and we are proud to play a part in Alvotech's ongoing growth and success. Ambitious multijurisdictional projects like this one is at the heart of what we do." says partner Charlotte Levin.

Our team was led by partner Charlotte Levin, managing associate Nima Naderi and associates Rick Cuperus and Albert Danielsson (Stockholm) and partner Mike Bienenfeld and law clerk Ariana Arzani (London).

For more information, please email linklatersstockholm@linklaters.com 

Linklaters is a leading global law firm, supporting and investing in the future of our clients wherever they do business. We combine legal expertise with a collaborative and innovative approach to help clients navigate constantly evolving markets and regulatory environments, pursuing opportunities and managing risk worldwide.

https://news.cision.com/se/linklaters/r/linklaters-has-advised-the-syndicate-of-banks-in-connection-with-alvotech-s-private-placement%2Cc4159783

data.title: Irish GDP jumps 9.7% q/q on pharma exports to US
data.description:
Ireland's gross domestic product grew by a hefty 9.7% quarter-on-quarter in the first three months of the year due to a surge in pharmaceutical exports to the U.S., likely further distorting the average growth rate across the euro zone.

With Ireland's large multinational sector often distorting GDP, officials prefer to use modified domestic demand (MDD) to more accurately gauge the strength of the economy.

MDD has expanded strongly in recent years and grew by 0.8% in the first quarter compared with the previous three months, Central Statistics Office (CSO) data showed on Thursday.

GDP has at times diverged sharply from activity in the wider economy and an initial estimate of a much lower quarterly growth of 3.6% had already inflated the euro zone average, which grew by a faster than expected 0.4% in the first quarter.

Irish GDP was 22.2% higher year-on-year compared to an initial estimate of +13.3%.

"This has come directly out of cross-border pharmaceutical exports in March, particularly," Christopher Sibley, Assistant Director General in the Central Statistics Office, told a news conference.

Separate data last month showed that exports of medical and pharmaceutical products surged by 154% or 34 billion euros in the first quarter, chiefly due to U.S. drugmakers stocking up ahead of potential import tariffs.

More than a dozen of the world's biggest pharma companies have plants in Ireland, where many make medicines or active ingredients for the U.S. market. Exports of pharmaceutical products to the U.S. rose by 243% alone in March.

Thursday's data showing a 14.8% quarter-on-quarter rise in goods exports and a 17.1% expansion in the industrial sector, which Sibley said showed that foreign multinationals were taking large profits from their export sales.

Ireland's finance ministry last month downgraded its growth forecasts slightly for modified domestic demand this year to 2% if a 10% tariff on European Union exports to the U.S. remains in place, or 2.5% if tariffs are removed.

The finance ministry marked up its forecast for GDP - even in the event of the tariffs sticking - to 4.0% from 3.9% before U.S. President Donald Trump's election last November.

data.title: Social Buzz: Wallstreetbets Stocks Mixed Premarket Thursday; Applied Digital to Advance, Tesla to Decline
data.description:
The most-talked-about stocks in the Reddit subforum Wallstreetbets were mixed hours before Thursday's opening bell.

Applied Digital advanced by 5.6% pre-bell, after closing Wednesday with a 29.3% increase.

Robinhood Markets rose by 1.5% premarket, following a 0.8% increase from the previous session.

Broadcom Inc. was up 1.4% in pre-bell activity, after a 1.7% increase from Wednesday's session.

UnitedHealth Group was 0.4% higher in premarket hours, swinging from a 0.3% decline at Wednesday's close.

Tesla was down 1.6% premarket, following a 3.6% decline from the previous session.

CoreWeave was 1% lower in pre-bell activity, swinging from an 8.4% increase at Wednesday's close.

Reddit fell by 0.7% pre-bell, after closing Wednesday with a 6.6% increase.

Nvidia slightly fell hours before market open, following a 0.5% increase from the previous session.

data.title: Beat Rejects Shareholder Proposal on Fundraising Terms
data.description:
Beat Holdings has rejected a shareholder proposal from the H.a.N Group, which called for a third-party allotment of new shares and stock acquisition rights at a 30% discount to market price.

The investment company cited concerns over potential excessive dilution of up to 296.65% and the risk of a change in control, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on Wednesday.

While Beat is exploring financing options to continue its Bitcoin ETF investments, it considers the terms of the proposal unfavorable.

data.title: Tosei Disposes of Tokyo Real Estate
data.description:
Tosei is disposing of the trust beneficiary rights, located at 2-chome, Hatchobori, Chuo-ku, Tokyo at an undisclosed value, according to its Tokyo bourse filing on Wednesday.

The property includes an office and parking, with a total floor area of 6,268.05 square meters, and the selling price exceeds 10% of the company's consolidated revenue for the fiscal year ended Nov. 30, 2024.

The contract is scheduled for June 6, 2025, with closing expected on Dec. 5, 2025, and the impact will be reflected in the fiscal year ending Nov. 30, 2026, consolidated business forecast.

data.title: Wise’s US listing switch lacks financial wisdom
data.description:
The $15 bln UK moneychanger may shift its main trading venue to gain more American investors and liquidity. It could achieve similar ends by staying put and pushing for FSTE inclusion. A premium valuation suggests little upside, and many pond-hoppers have badly underperformed.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Karen Kwok on LinkedIn and X.

CONTEXT NEWS

Wise on June 5 said it intended to transfer its primary listing to a U.S. stock exchange and maintain a secondary listing in London. The UK-based money-transfer company said that doing so would expand the pool of its possible shareholder to include American domestic institutional and retail investors.

Its share rose 9% to 1,180 pence as of 0826 GMT on June 5.

data.title: Egypt, Greece agree to protect status of Mount Sinai monastery, after court ruling
data.description:
Greece and Egypt have agreed to safeguard the status of one of the world's oldest sites of Christian worship, foreign ministers of both countries said late on Wednesday, after an Egyptian court ruling last week cast uncertainty over its future.

The St Catherine's Monastery, at the foot of Egypt's Mount Sinai, was founded in the 6th century and is the oldest Christian monastery still in use for its original function, says UNESCO, which has listed the area as a World Heritage site.

Revered by Christians, Muslims and Jews, the monastery is at the site where by Biblical tradition Moses received the Ten Commandments.

But last week, an Egyptian court ruling seen by Reuters ordered Orthodox monks to vacate several plots of land that the monks have used for years, including vineyards and gardens adjacent to the monastery compound, on the grounds that they were illegally sequestered, prompting a diplomatic flurry between Cairo and Athens over the site's status.

"We agreed in the immediate future to work towards safeguarding the rights of the monastery, as well as its legal status," Greek Foreign Minister George Gerapetritis said after meeting his Egyptian counterpart Badr Abdelatty in Cairo.

"Both Egypt and Greece intend to move forward based on the long-standing tradition and the already established status of an emblematic monastery for its Greek Orthodox character of worship".

Abdelatty said that the ruling preserves the monastery’s profound spiritual value and religious standing, and confirmed that the monks would continue to have access to and use of the monastery and its religious and historical sites, according to a foreign ministry statement.

With a long history of diplomatic ties, Greece and Egypt have deepened cooperation in recent years.

St Catherine's is a sprawling complex, and according to tradition it was built around a burning bush where God was said to have spoken to Moses as described in the Book of Exodus. Its library is one of the most extensive worldwide, containing some of the world's earliest Christian manuscripts.

data.title: MVW signs Green De-Oiler Technology Trial Agreement with a Major O&G Producer in the Sultanate of Oman
data.description:
Bergen, Norway, June 5, 2025 – M Vest Water (EURONEXT: MVW) announces that a major Oil & Gas producer in Oman has decided to qualify NORWAFLOC®. The qualification will take place at the oil field, which is the largest Produced Water contributor in Oman.

The large-scale trial will be conducted over a two-week period in Q3 2025. During this trial, a Produced Water stream of 110,000 m3/day will be treated by replacing incumbent chemicals with our patented, nature-based NORWAFLOC® de-oiler and demulsifier. Upon meeting the operator’s treatment success criteria, MVW’s environmentally friendly NORWAFLOC® chemical may be qualified for use in this major oilfield and within the operator's Produced Water activities in general.

The field where the trial takes place has a total Produced Water production of 240,000 m3/day and is a reputed flagship in sustainable Produced Water management. Approximately half of this Produced Water is used to build the largest constructed wetland in the world, where the cultivation of vegetation results in the greening of the desert and the creation of new habitats for birds, fish and wildlife. At the same time, the natural vegetation filters and cleans the Produced Water. This sustainable method of handling the Produced Water developed in this oilfield has garnered multiple awards and headlines around the world.

“We are very pleased that the client has chosen to qualify MVW’s products at this prestigious oil field, and thereby giving MVW the opportunity to add value to this sustainable showpiece our client has created within Produced Water management. With our nature-based NORWAFLOC®, we believe we can contribute to this customers’ high sustainability goals and achieve substantially increased oil reclamation, and implicit revenue gains, in the oil-water separation before it goes to the wetland treatment,” says Tor Olav Gabrielsen, Executive Chairman at MVW.

data.title: Commerzbank on Overnight News
data.description:
Commerzbank in its "European Sunrise" note of Thursday highlighted:

Markets: United States Treasuries close near highs, slightly better offered in Asia. Japanese Government Bonds stage long-end rally despite weak 30-year auction. E-minis weaken while Asian stocks are mixed. EUR trades around $1.141. Brent remains below $65/barrel after OPEC+ headline.

Fed: Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari says the Fed should stay in wait-and-see mode as the labor market is showing signs of slowing down, while inflation isn't back at target. Senate confirms Michelle Bowman as Fed Vice Chair for Supervision.

Beige Book reports that economic activity has declined while prices increased at a "moderate" pace. Expectation of rising costs and prices are widespread, with a few regions expecting these to be "strong, significant and substantial."

U.S.: White House bans travel from 12 countries, President Donald Trump signs executive order banning foreign students from Harvard University.

U.S.: Trump says he thinks the debt limit should be eliminated. CBO says "big beautiful bill" would add $2.4 trillion to U.S. debt by 2034, while tariffs would shrink deficit by $2.8 trillion over a decade.

Saudi Arabia wants to continue OPEC+ supply hikes by at least 411,000 barrels/day in August and possibly September, according to Bloomberg sources.

NATO: U.S. ambassador Matthew Whitaker says that all members must agree to spend 5% of gross domestic product on defense.

Russia-Ukraine war: Trump predicts "no immediate peace" after call with Russian President Vladimir Putin, Putin says Russia has to respond to the Ukraine drone attack.

==EUROPE:

Germany: Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil's chief adviser supports joint issuance for defense, and in the more distant future, as general financing basis, provided it is well justified as the European Union evolves into sovereign.

==ASIA:

China: Caixin Services PMI rises 0.4pts to 51.1 while consensus was 51.0.

data.title: American Electric Power Unit PSO to Acquire Green Country Power Plant
data.description:
American Electric Power's unit, Public Service Company of Oklahoma, said Thursday it received approval from the Oklahoma Corporation Commission to acquire the 795 MW Green Country Power Plant in Jenks, Oklahoma.

No financial details were provided.

PSO said the natural gas-fired facility will be added to its generation portfolio by July to support rising demand and improve grid reliability.

data.title: Vermilion Energy to sell US assets for $120 million
data.description: Canada's Vermilion Energy said on Thursday it will sell its United States assets for $120 million.

data.description: Amphenol has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $91.19, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Silver miners rise as white metal's prices hit 13-year high
data.description:
** U.S.-listed shares of silver miners up premarket, tracking white metal's rising prices

** Spot silver up 3.1% at $35.58/ounce, its highest level since February 2012

** Silver prices rose above key $35/ounce level for first time since October 2012 as investors look forward to U.S. non-farm payrolls data due later this week to assess country's interest-rate path

** Hecla Mining and Coeur Mining rise 4.3% and 1.8%, respectively

** Canadian miners Endeavour Silver , and Silvercorp Metals , up 5.4% and 3.3%, respectively

** Abrdn Physical Silver Shares ETF and iShares Silver Trust up 3.3% and 3.3%, respectively

data.title: AMMB to Replace Hong Leong Financial in Malaysia's Benchmark Stock Index
data.description:
By Ying Xian Wong

AMMB Holdings will replace Hong Leong Financial Group on the list of component stocks in Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index.

The changes to the FTSE Bursa Malaysia Kuala Lumpur Composite Index will take effect on June 23, Bursa Malaysia and FTSE Russell said Thursday after a semiannual review.

The index's reserve list includes Genting, IOI Properties, KPJ Healthcare, United Plantations and Westports Holdings, the five largest non-index constituents by market capitalization, the stock exchange and FTSE Russell said.

KLCI constituents must be among the 30 largest companies in Malaysia by market capitalization and meet free-float as well as liquidity requirements.

Hong Leong Financial failed the liquidity screening test, meeting the required trading velocity in only seven of the 12 months ended May, short of the eight-month threshold, CIMB Securities said in a recent note.

Lender AMMB ended 0.7% higher at 5.38 ringgit, bringing its market capitalization to 17.70 billion ringgit, equivalent to $4.17 billion.

Write to Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com

data.title: Palladium is up by 5.06%
data.description: Palladium increased 5.06% to 1038.5 USD/t.oz

data.title: Irish Current Account Surplus Narrows in Q1
data.description:
The current account surplus in Ireland narrowed to EUR 6.6 billion in the first quarter of 2025 from EUR 14.9 billion in the corresponding period of 2024.

The services account posted a deficit of EUR 0.4 billion, switching from a EUR 9.3 billion surplus.

Also, the primary income gap widened to EUR 66.2 billion from EUR 32 billion and the secondary income shortfall was little changed at EUR 1 billion.

Meanwhile, the goods surplus widened to EUR 74.2 billion from EUR 38.8 billion.
